ddls:
CREATE TABLE "respondents" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the respondent */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent's gender */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent's country */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the respondent */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the respondent */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the respondent */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the respondent's record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the respondent's record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "poverty_perceptions" (
  "perception_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each perception entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "poverty_level" TEXT /* Perceived level of poverty (e.g., Too Little, About Right, Too Much) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments from the respondent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("perception_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_poverty_perceptions_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "religions" (
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each religion entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "religion" TEXT /* Religious affiliation (e.g., Christianity, Islam, etc.) */,
  "practicing" INTEGER /* Whether the respondent practices their religion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("religion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_religions_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "education_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent's education type */,
  "degree" TEXT /* Degree held by the respondent (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, etc.) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution where the respondent studied */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_levels_education_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_type_id") REFERENCES education_types ("education_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_levels_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demo_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent's age group */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent's gender */,
  "income" INTEGER /* Annual income of the respondent */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the respondent (e.g., employed, unemployed, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demo_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey result */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "poverty_level" TEXT /* Perceived level of poverty */,
  "religion" TEXT /* Religious affiliation */,
  "degree" TEXT /* Degree held by the respondent */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments from the respondent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_results_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the survey question */,
  "question_type" TEXT /* Type of the question (e.g., multiple choice, open-ended, etc.) */,
  "options" TEXT /* Options for multiple-choice questions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response entry */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* ID of the question being answered */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent providing the answer */,
  "response" TEXT /* Text of the response given by the respondent */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_question_id FOREIGN KEY ("question_id") REFERENCES survey_questions ("question_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Description of the age group (e.g., 18-24, 25-34) */,
  "min_age" INTEGER /* Minimum age for the age group */,
  "max_age" INTEGER /* Maximum age for the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Definition of the gender (e.g., male, female) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_types" (
  "education_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education type */,
  "education_type" TEXT /* Description of the type of education (e.g., high school, bachelor's) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validation" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation rule */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table to which the rule applies */,
  "column_name" TEXT /* Name of the column to which the rule applies */,
  "rule" TEXT /* Validation rule (e.g., NOT NULL, UNIQUE, etc.) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Error message to display when the rule is violated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table that was changed */,
  "column_name" TEXT /* Name of the column that was changed */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the column */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the column */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "changed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the change was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
